seo.title: Building Content Generation with Amazon Bedrock Agents + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build content generation with Amazon Bedrock Agents + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Amazon Bedrock Agents enable powerful LLM-driven content generation workflows with built-in knowledge bases, action groups, and guardrails for enterprise-scale deployments. However, risks like brand voice drift, factual inaccuracies, and policy violations can undermine output quality and compliance. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure generated content meets brand consistency, factual accuracy, and editorial standards before delivery.

architectureOverview.heading: Content Generation Architecture with Amazon Bedrock Agents
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture centers on a Bedrock Agent configured with a knowledge base containing brand guidelines, style guides, and verified facts. Action groups handle research via Lambda functions that query internal databases or external APIs. User requests trigger agent orchestration, retrieving relevant chunks from the knowledge base, executing actions for fact-checking, and applying guardrails before final content synthesis.

Multi-turn conversations maintain session state for iterative refinement, with pre/post-processing steps enforcing SEO compliance and originality checks. The agent leverages foundation models like Claude or Llama, customized with prompt engineering for brand voice.

Vex integrates as a post-processing layer, wrapping `invoke_agent()` responses to verify outputs against domain-specific criteria before client delivery.

risks.name: Action group Lambda functions returning malformed responses
risks.description: Lambda functions for fact-checking or SEO analysis return JSON with missing fields or invalid formats, causing agent parsing failures
risks.mitigation: Wrap Lambda responses with structured validation schemas in Vex.verify()

risks.name: Knowledge base retrieval missing relevant documents due to chunking
risks.description: Brand guidelines split across chunks fail to retrieve complete context, leading to inconsistent tone
risks.mitigation: Verify retrieved context coverage against query intent with semantic similarity checks

risks.name: Agent guardrails not catching domain-specific policy violations
risks.description: Generic guardrails miss brand-specific prohibitions like competitor mentions or outdated messaging
risks.mitigation: Layer Vex custom policies for domain-specific brand compliance

risks.name: Session state loss between multi-turn agent invocations
risks.description: Context drift in iterative content refinement causes brand voice inconsistency across turns
risks.mitigation: Track session state continuity with Vex memory verification

vexIntegration.title: Python Vex Verification Post-Processing
vexIntegration.description: Wrap agent response with comprehensive content verification
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import verify, Policy

content_policies = [
    Policy.brand_consistency(),
    Policy.factual_accuracy(),
    Policy.originality_check(),
    Policy.seo_compliance(target_keywords=['brand', 'product']),
    Policy.tone_adherence('professional, engaging, authoritative')
]

def verified_content_generation(request: str, session_id: str) -> dict:
    # Invoke agent
    response = invoke_content_agent(request, session_id)
    content = extract_content_completion(response)
    
    # Vex verification
    result = verify(
        content,
        policies=content_policies,
        context={'session_id': session_id, 'request': request}
    )
    
    return {
        'content': content,
        'verified': result.passed,
        'issues': result.issues,
        'score': result.score
    }
